supply_chain.dependencies: Dependence on third-party service providers for significant portions of systems-related support, equipment, facilities, and certain data, including data center operations, data network, voice communication services and pharmacy data processing
supply_chain.risks: Dependence on independent third parties makes operations vulnerable to third parties' failure to perform adequately; change in service providers could result in decline in service quality and effectiveness or less favorable contract terms

regulatory_risks: Changes to Medicare Advantage and Prescription Drug Plans programs due to legislative or regulatory action
Reductions in premium payments from CMS
Increases in member benefits or changes to member eligibility criteria without corresponding increases in premium payments
Changes to Part D prescription drug benefit design under the Inflation Reduction Act
Restrictions on ability to manage provider network, market and sell products, or otherwise operate business
Restrictions on profitability, including reviews by regulatory bodies comparing Medicare Advantage profitability to non-Medicare Advantage business profitability
Further restrictions on service arrangements and fee payments between intercompany or vertically-integrated assets
Increases in regulation of prescription drug benefit businesses
CMS Risk-Adjustment Data Validation (RADV) audits and elimination of Fee-for-Service Adjuster (FFS Adjuster)
State insurance and health care regulatory authority reviews and investigations
Health Care Reform Law requirements including minimum benefit ratios and rebates to policyholders
HIPAA and HITECH Act privacy and security regulations
Corporate Practice of Medicine and fee-splitting state laws
Anti-Kickback Statute, Stark Law, and False Claims Act compliance
State Certificate of Need (CON) requirements for healthcare services expansion
State regulations on payment of dividends and minimum capital requirements for insurance subsidiaries
Pharmacy regulation at state and federal levels including DEA registration and FDA requirements
USPS restrictions on transmission of drugs through mail
U.S. Department of Transportation regulations on drug distribution
